differential_diagnoses: Acute kidney injury (likely acute tubular necrosis or cortical necrosis)
next_steps: Remove the tourniquet after establishing proximal venous access
Administer polyvalent antivenom indicated for the identified snake species
Initiate fluid resuscitation and administer blood products (packed red blood cells, fresh frozen plasma, and platelets)
Perform immediate obstetrical consultation for potential emergency cesarean delivery given maternal instability and fetal distress
Consult nephrology for potential urgent hemodialysis due to severe uremia and hypercreatininemia
Monitor fetal heart rate continuously and perform serial physical examinations for uterine tone
Administer tetanus prophylaxis and broad-spectrum antibiotics for potential wound infection
